title: Zhang 2022 – FerrDB Role Counts
text:
Cumulative FerrDB hits by rank
For each gene list, genes are already ordered by decreasing absolute score. The plot shows the cumulative count of genes classified as driver, suppressor, or marker in FerrDB as a function of rank. A steeper curve indicates that high-scoring genes are more enriched for ferroptosis-relevant roles.

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheet imported: Supplementary Table 1.

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
No row filtering applied.


Rows: 18054

title: Zhang 2022 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Plots

Volcano plot
This plot replicates figure 1a (right panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Volcano plot for the 72-hour Erastin treatment condition. Genes highlighted in red (PCBP1, GPX4, ACSL4, KEAP1, PKCβ) are those labelled by the original authors in their figure, used here as reference points to confirm the data was imported and processed correctly. The dashed line marks p-value = 0.001, the threshold applied below. Dots appearing at the top edge of the plot have p-values below 10^-6 (the upper limit of the y-axis); any with p = 0 represent numerical infinities (–log10(0) = ∞).

text:

$zhang_2022_crispr_low_score

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheet imported: Table S1 Gene Scores.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
Remove rows whose Names entry starts with sg (guide RNA controls) or starts with INTERGENIC (non-gene features).


Rows before filtering: 23940 


Rows after filtering:  2997

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Rank genes
Genes ranked by ascending score in each condition.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:
Plots

Cyto score ranked plot
This plot replicates figure 2D (left panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Genes ranked by ascending cytoplasmic gshF score under BSO treatment. GCLM is highlighted as a positive control — it encodes the modifier subunit of glutamate-cysteine ligase, a key enzyme in glutathione biosynthesis. The dashed lines mark the ±1.5 threshold used to extract the high- and low-score gene lists below.







Mito score ranked plot
This plot replicates figure 2D (right panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Same as above for the mitochondrial gshF condition. GCLM is again highlighted as a reference point.







Cyto rank vs mito rank (log-log)
This plot replicates figure 2C (left panel) from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
Cross-condition comparison of gene ranks on log scales. Genes in the lower-left corner score poorly (low rank) in both conditions, suggesting relevance to glutathione homeostasis regardless of compartment. Highlighted genes are known those labelled by the original authors in their figure, used here as reference points to confirm the data was imported and processed correctly.

title: Timson 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene Lists Extraction
text:

$timson_2024_cyto_low_score

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Load data
Exclude ferrdb_disease.csv (no gene information) and ferrdb_inducer.csv and ferrdb_inhibitor.csv (document substances, not genes). Filter each file to ferroptosis entries only via the RCD column.

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Bar plot
Each bar shows the number of distinct genes annotated with that role in FerrDB for ferroptosis. The count label is printed above each bar for easy reference.

title: FerrDb – Known Ferroptosis Modulators extraction
text:
Export


Wrote 1957 rows to data/ferrdb_genes.tsv

title: Ferroptosis Data Extraction
text:
FerrDB v3: Used to extract known ferroptososis modulators and their roles.




Published CRISPR datasets are used to extract lists of candidate genes for meta-analysis.



Cross-checking extracted gene lists against FerrDB v3 for known ferroptosis modulators.

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Import data
Sheets imported: Cys Starv_Cys 0µM and Cys Starv_Cys 1µM.

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Filter to genes only
No row filtering applied.


Rows (0 µM): 18423 


Rows (1 µM): 18449

title: Yang 2024 – CRISPR Screen Gene List Extraction
text:
Plots

Beta score scatter: 0 µM vs 1 µM
This plot replicates figure S1B from the original publication, confirming that the data was imported and processed correctly.
MAGeCK beta scores for each gene plotted across the two cysteine conditions (0 µM vs 1 µM). Genes with consistently negative beta scores in both conditions — appearing in the lower-left quadrant — are candidates whose loss is detrimental under cysteine limitation regardless of its severity. Highlighted genes (red) are those labelled by the original authors.







Volcano plot: 0 µM cysteine
Custom plot to inform the selection of thresholds for candidate gene extraction. Highlighted genes (red) are the same as those labelled in the beta score scatter above, as selected by the original authors. The dashed line marks p-value = 0.001, the threshold applied below. Dots stacked along the top edge of the plot represent genes with a p-value of exactly 0, i.e. –log10(0) = ∞.







Volcano plot: 1 µM cysteine
Same as above for the low cysteine (1 µM) condition. The dashed line marks p-value = 0.001, the threshold applied below.

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
This notebook combines all extracted gene lists from notebooks in ../data-extraction/ (excluding index.qmd) into one table and one heatmap.

Rows represent genes.
Columns represent individual extracted lists.
TRUE (or a colored tile) means that gene appears in that list.

This gives a quick visual summary of overlap and differences across studies.

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
This notebook combines all extracted gene lists from notebooks in ../data-extraction/ (excluding index.qmd) into one table and one heatmap.

Rows represent genes.
Columns represent individual extracted lists.
TRUE (or a colored tile) means that gene appears in that list.

This gives a quick visual summary of overlap and differences across studies.

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
Build the Gene-by-List Logical Matrix
The table below has one row per gene and one column per extracted list. Each cell is TRUE if that gene appears in that list and FALSE otherwise.


Warning: package 'yaml' was built under R version 4.5.2


Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2


Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2


# A tibble: 524 × 11
   gene   timson_2024_cyto_high_…¹ timson_2024_cyto_low…² timson_2024_mito_hig…³
   <chr>  <lgl>                    <lgl>                  <lgl>                 
 1 KEAP1  FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 2 LIPT1  FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 3 NDUFA1 FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 4 NDUFA2 FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 5 NDUFS1 FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 6 PAICS  FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 7 RRM1   FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
 8 SEPHS2 FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE

10 ASNA1  FALSE                    TRUE                   FALSE                 
# ℹ 514 more rows

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
Heatmap View
The heatmap below visualises the same matrix: green tiles indicate that the gene (row) is present in that extracted list (column). Gene labels are hidden on the y-axis to keep the figure readable at scale.

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
Study Hit Counts per Gene
A gene appearing in more than one study provides stronger cross-study evidence for a role in ferroptosis regulation. The table below counts, for each gene, how many distinct studies include it in at least one extracted list. A gene is counted once per study regardless of how many lists within that study contain it. List IDs follow the {author}_{year}_... convention, so the study is taken as the first two underscore-delimited tokens.


# A tibble: 524 × 2
   gene    n_studies
   <chr>       <int>
 1 KEAP1           3
 2 RRM1            3
 3 ALDOA           2
 4 ASNA1           2
 5 CDIPT           2
 6 CNEP1R1         2
 7 COQ3            2
 8 COX5B           2
 9 FXN             2
10 GLRX5           2
# ℹ 514 more rows


The table below summarises how many genes are supported by exactly 1, 2, 3, … independent studies.


# A tibble: 3 × 2
  n_studies n_genes
      <int>   <int>
1         3       2
2         2      35
3         1     487

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
section: Gene List Hit Counts per Gene
text:
Gene List Hit Counts per Gene
The table below counts how many individual gene lists (across all studies and conditions) contain each gene. Unlike the section above, no deduplication by study is applied: a gene appearing in four lists from the same study contributes 4 to its count. This is useful when lists within a study are not independent — for example, when the same screen is thresholded at multiple conditions — but the totals should be interpreted with that caveat in mind.


# A tibble: 524 × 2
   gene   n_lists
   <chr>    <int>
 1 KEAP1        4
 2 LIPT1        4
 3 NDUFA1       4
 4 NDUFA2       4
 5 NDUFS1       4
 6 PAICS        4
 7 RRM1         4
 8 SEPHS2       4
 9 ALDOA        3
10 ASNA1        3
# ℹ 514 more rows


The table below summarises how many genes appear in exactly 1, 2, 3, … individual lists.


# A tibble: 4 × 2
  n_lists n_genes
    <int>   <int>
1       4       8
2       3      22
3       2     151
4       1     343

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
Gene–Study–List Reference Table
The table below is a long-format reference: one row per (gene, gene list) combination, limited to genes that are present in at least one list. The study column is derived from the first two tokens of the list ID (e.g. timson_2024). Use this table to look up, for any gene of interest, which studies and which specific lists it was identified in.


# A tibble: 743 × 3
   gene     study       gene_list                   
   <chr>    <chr>       <chr>                       
 1 AASDHPPT timson_2024 timson_2024_mito_low_score  
 2 ABCB7    timson_2024 timson_2024_cyto_low_score  
 3 ABCB7    timson_2024 timson_2024_mito_low_score  
 4 ABCC1    zhang_2022  zhang_2022_crispr_high_score
 5 ABCC6    zhang_2022  zhang_2022_crispr_high_score
 6 ABCE1    timson_2024 timson_2024_cyto_low_score  
 7 ABCE1    timson_2024 timson_2024_mito_low_score  
 8 ABCF1    timson_2024 timson_2024_cyto_low_score  
 9 ABCF1    timson_2024 timson_2024_mito_low_score  
10 ABHD11   timson_2024 timson_2024_mito_low_score  
# ℹ 733 more rows

title: All Studies Gene List Overlap
text:
Excel Export
A CSV file intended for browsing and filtering in Excel. Each row is a gene; columns are: gene, n_studies, n_lists, then one logical column per gene list. Genes are ordered by n_studies descending, then n_lists descending, then alphabetically.


Wrote 524 genes to data/gene_by_list.csv
